title: tanacetum alpinum var. tatrae
intro: Tanacetum alpinum var. tatrae, also known as Alpine Tansy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family native to the Carpathian Mountains. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to its essential oils and sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Tanacetum alpinum var. tatrae include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of the common cold and flu. Tanacetum alpinum var. tatrae can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and support the immune system.
